predictions.title: Open-weight 1M-context era arrives as the new floor by Q3 2026
predictions.body: **The "1M context as default" open-weight era arrives in full by Q3 2026.** DeepSeek V4's "1M context designed in, not bolted on," Qwen3.6-27B's 262K → 1M extension, Claude Opus 4.7's 1M context (Bedrock), and GPT-5.5's long-context strengthening all point the same direction. By Q3 2026, "native 1M context" becomes the floor for open weights, and existing 32K-128K models drop to legacy. RAG pipelines flip from "search → context-insert" to "dump everything in + dynamic prune," and vector DB vendors are pushed to redefine themselves as "long-context cache optimization layers." In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

reasoning.because: DeepSeek V4 ships native 1M context, Qwen3.6-27B extends 262K to 1M, Claude Opus 4.7 hits 1M on Bedrock, and GPT-5.5 strengthens long context.
reasoning.given: Hybrid attention drops 1M-context FLOPs to 27% and KV cache to 10% of V3.2, making 1M context affordable in open weights.
reasoning.so_that: Native 1M context becomes the open-weight floor and RAG flips from search-then-insert to dump-everything-plus-dynamic-prune workflows.
reasoning.landing: By Q3 2026; 32K-128K open-weight models drop to legacy and vector-DB vendors reposition.
reasoning.plain_language: Big AI models can now read a whole library at once, and cheap open-weight versions are catching up. Soon any model under that size will look outdated.

predictions.summary: DeepSeek V4's native 1M context, Qwen3.6-27B's 262K to 1M extension, Claude Opus 4.7's 1M on Bedrock, and GPT-5.5's long-context push all converge. By Q3 2026, native 1M context is expected to become the open-weight floor and RAG pipelines flip from search-and-insert to dump-and-prune.

predictions.title: Physical AI 8-hour production runs become enterprise RFP cuts by Q4 2026
predictions.body: **"Physical AI 8-hour production runs" become an enterprise procurement requirement in H2 2026.** Siemens-Humanoid HMND 01 Alpha's **8 hours / 90% success / 60 totes/hour** at Erlangen — on the production line, not a demo — is a clear graduation point from "humanoid = PoC." By Q4 2026, big-manufacturer RFPs (VW / Bosch / Mercedes / Foxconn / TSMC / Toyota) will fold "24h continuous operation," "1-week MTBF," "90%+ autonomous completion rate," and "MES / PLC integration" into de facto cuts. The competitive axis for Physical AI shifts entirely from "good-looking demo videos" to "ease of insertion into existing OT environments." In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

reasoning.because: Siemens-Humanoid HMND 01 Alpha logs 8 hours / 60 totes per hour / 90% success on the Erlangen production line, not a demo.
reasoning.given: Big-manufacturer procurement teams now have a concrete production benchmark to anchor RFP cuts against humanoid PoC vendors.
reasoning.so_that: VW / Bosch / Mercedes / Foxconn / TSMC / Toyota RFPs adopt 24h continuous operation, 1-week MTBF, 90% autonomy, and MES / PLC integration as de facto cuts.
reasoning.landing: By Q4 2026; competitive axis shifts to OT-environment insertion ease.
reasoning.plain_language: A factory humanoid just worked 8 hours straight on a real production line. Soon big manufacturers will only buy robots that pass that bar.

predictions.summary: Siemens-Humanoid HMND 01 Alpha's 8-hour Erlangen run at 60 totes/hour and 90% success on a live production line graduates humanoids from PoC. By Q4 2026, big-manufacturer RFPs are expected to fold 24h continuous operation, 1-week MTBF, 90% autonomy, and MES / PLC integration into procurement cuts.

predictions.title: Capital markets reprice agent-displaced SaaS by agent-touched API share Q2 2026
predictions.body: **Capital markets reset their valuation of "AI-agent-displaced enterprise SaaS" inside Q2 2026.** ServiceNow's **-18% (worst single-day drop ever)** on Apr 23, IBM's same-day decline, and the chained falls of Salesforce / Workday / Oracle reveal a shift in how capital markets weigh "AI cost-savings benefit" vs "AI displacement of legacy workflow SaaS." Inside Q2 2026, expect Gartner / Forrester Magic Quadrant / Wave reports to make the "Agent-native vs Legacy SaaS" axis explicit, and market-cap math to move from "seats × ARPU" to "share of API calls touched by agents." Lagging analyst consensus updates mean another 3-5 names (Workday / Atlassian / Box / Smartsheet etc.) likely see 15%+ drops in the next two quarters. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

reasoning.because: ServiceNow's worst-ever single-day -18% on Apr 23, IBM same-day decline, and chained Salesforce / Workday / Oracle falls expose the displacement axis.
reasoning.given: Capital markets now weigh AI displacement of legacy workflow SaaS against AI cost savings, repricing seat-based revenue.
reasoning.so_that: Gartner / Forrester make Agent-native vs Legacy SaaS explicit and market-cap math moves from seats x ARPU to share of agent-touched API calls.
reasoning.landing: Inside Q2 2026; another 3-5 names likely take 15%+ drops over two quarters.
reasoning.plain_language: ServiceNow's stock had its worst day ever because investors think AI agents will replace its software. Other workflow SaaS firms are next.

predictions.summary: ServiceNow's worst-ever -18% single-day drop on Apr 23, IBM's same-day fall, and chained Salesforce / Workday / Oracle declines mark a capital-markets reset on AI-agent displacement of legacy SaaS. Inside Q2 2026, Gartner / Forrester are expected to make the Agent-native vs Legacy axis explicit and 3-5 more names likely see 15%+ drops.
